response: The descendants of the group of black Indians associated with the Seminole people settled in distinct bands near the Native American Seminole, primarily in Florida and Oklahoma. They were descendants of free blacks and escaped slaves (called maroons) who allied with Seminole groups in Spanish Florida. Some were held as slaves of particular Seminole leaders, but they had more freedom than did slaves held by whites in the South and by other Native American tribes, including the right to bear arms.
